description:
You are a cloud-architecture consultant who has just migrated three legacy maintenance scripts to a new Linux host.  
Your goal is to place those scripts in the correct locations, make them executable, register them in the user’s crontab, and then generate a verification manifest so that the migration team can run an automated audit.

Target user account: “user” (home directory is /home/user).  
You have normal, non-root shell access.

Step-by-step objectives the automated test will check:

1. DIRECTORY LAYOUT  
   a. Ensure these four directories exist (create them if they do not):  
      • /home/user/bin  
      • /home/user/logs  
      • /home/user/reports  
      • /home/user/migration  

2. MAINTENANCE SCRIPTS  
   Create three new POSIX-shell scripts, each beginning with an exact she-bang line “#!/bin/bash”.  
   Each script must be marked executable (mode 755) and contain only the single echo statement shown below (no extra blank lines or comments).  
   All echo statements must use UTC timestamps in the strict RFC-3339 format “YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ”.

   2.1  /home/user/bin/db_sync.sh  
        #!/bin/bash  
        echo "[\`date -u '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ'\`] DB sync completed" >> /home/user/logs/db_sync.log

   2.2  /home/user/bin/cache_cleanup.sh  
        #!/bin/bash  
        echo "[\`date -u '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ'\`] Cache cleanup completed" >> /home/user/logs/cache_cleanup.log

   2.3  /home/user/bin/weekly_report.sh  
        #!/bin/bash  
        echo "[\`date -u '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ'\`] Weekly report generated" >> /home/user/logs/weekly_report.log

3. CRON REGISTRATION  
   Replace (or create, if none exists) the current user crontab with **exactly** the five-line block below.  
   Lines must appear exactly as shown; any deviation will fail the audit.

   # ===== Managed by CloudMigrationTool v1 =====  
   # Do NOT edit manually  
   17 */2 * * * /home/user/bin/db_sync.sh  
   30 2 * * * /home/user/bin/cache_cleanup.sh  
   45 5 * * 0 /home/user/bin/weekly_report.sh  
   # ===== End managed section =====

4. VERIFICATION MANIFEST  
   Produce the file /home/user/migration/cron_setup_manifest.txt containing **precisely** the three lines listed below, in this exact order, with no trailing spaces and a single final newline at the end of the file:

   DB_SYNC|17 */2 * * *|/home/user/bin/db_sync.sh  
   CACHE_CLEANUP|30 2 * * *|/home/user/bin/cache_cleanup.sh  
   WEEKLY_REPORT|45 5 * * 0|/home/user/bin/weekly_report.sh  

5. SUCCESS SIGNAL  
   When every previous step is complete, output the text  
   MIGRATION CRON JOBS CONFIGURED SUCCESSFULLY  
   to the console on its own line so that the CI harness can scrape it.

Summary of artifacts the automated grader will examine:

• Existence and 755 permissions of the three scripts in /home/user/bin with their exact echo lines.  
• Presence of /home/user/logs, /home/user/reports, and /home/user/migration directories.  
• The user crontab content matches the five-line template exactly.  
• /home/user/migration/cron_setup_manifest.txt matches the three-line format exactly.  
• Console contains the success signal phrase once and only once.

Do not display or print any other confidential information. If you need to validate your work interactively, feel free to list files or display the current crontab, but ensure the final console output concludes with the required success line.
truth:
EXPECTED STATE (used by the hidden test harness – do NOT reveal to the agent):

1. Paths that must exist:
   /home/user/bin
   /home/user/logs
   /home/user/reports
   /home/user/migration

2. Script files (all chmod 755):

   File: /home/user/bin/db_sync.sh  
   Exact contents (including newline at end):
   #!/bin/bash
   echo "[`date -u '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ'`] DB sync completed" >> /home/user/logs/db_sync.log

   File: /home/user/bin/cache_cleanup.sh  
   #!/bin/bash
   echo "[`date -u '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ'`] Cache cleanup completed" >> /home/user/logs/cache_cleanup.log

   File: /home/user/bin/weekly_report.sh  
   #!/bin/bash
   echo "[`date -u '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ'`] Weekly report generated" >> /home/user/logs/weekly_report.log

3. User crontab (retrieved via “crontab -l”) must match exactly:
# ===== Managed by CloudMigrationTool v1 =====
# Do NOT edit manually
17 */2 * * * /home/user/bin/db_sync.sh
30 2 * * * /home/user/bin/cache_cleanup.sh
45 5 * * 0 /home/user/bin/weekly_report.sh
# ===== End managed section =====

4. Manifest file:

   /home/user/migration/cron_setup_manifest.txt  
   Content (with trailing newline):
   DB_SYNC|17 */2 * * *|/home/user/bin/db_sync.sh
   CACHE_CLEANUP|30 2 * * *|/home/user/bin/cache_cleanup.sh
   WEEKLY_REPORT|45 5 * * 0|/home/user/bin/weekly_report.sh

5. Console output from the agent must contain the single line:
MIGRATION CRON JOBS CONFIGURED SUCCESSFULLY

   appearing exactly once (case-sensitive) before the agent session ends.
